steps.title: Use zero product property
steps.content: The equation is already factored. A product equals $0$ when at least one factor equals $0$.

steps.title: List the roots
steps.content:
Set each factor equal to $0$.
$$x=0,\quad x=13,\quad x=-6,\quad x=5$$
The squared factor still gives $x=5$.

steps.title: Pick the greatest root
steps.content: Compare the four roots. The greatest root is $13$. The answer is 13.
